serving.natural_language_portion: A standard 330 ml can of Coca-Cola, equivalent to approximately 346 g, is the most common single-serve portion consumed in Singapore and globally.

nutrient_source.title: Coca-Cola (carbonated soft drink) — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Coca-Cola carbonated soft drink, matching the input record with confidence 0.95. USDA FoodData Central NDB No. 14400 (Carbonated beverage, cola, contains caffeine) cross-referenced for secondary validation; values are consistent.

gi_source.title: Soft drink, cola, Coca-Cola — Glycemic Index Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 63 (medium) for Coca-Cola (regular, sugar-sweetened) is directly reported in the University of Sydney GI Database. Glycemic load per 330 ml serving calculated as: GI (63) × available carbohydrate per serving (35.6 g from 10.36 g/100g × 3.46) / 100 = 22.5 GL units.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (9.94 g/100 g) in a sugar-sweetened beverage independently raises blood pressure risk via insulin-driven sodium retention and weight gain; caffeine content adds acute pressor effect
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should avoid regular Coca-Cola entirely; a 330 ml can delivers approximately 34 g of free sugar and caffeine (~34 mg), both of which acutely and chronically elevate blood pressure — substitute with water, plain sparkling water, or Coca-Cola Zero Sugar.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Rapidly absorbed liquid sugar (9.94 g/100 g, GI 63) with zero dietary fibre causes rapid postprandial glucose spike; a 330 ml can delivers ~34 g of free sugar with no buffering
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid regular Coca-Cola; a single 330 ml can raises blood glucose by approximately 2–3 mmol/L and provides no nutritional benefit — switch to Coca-Cola Zero Sugar or plain water.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages is independently associated with increased risk of cardiovascular events, dyslipidaemia, and visceral adiposity; Coca-Cola provides 42 kcal/100 g as pure sugar with no protective nutrients
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should eliminate regular Coca-Cola from their diet; habitual SSB consumption raises triglycerides, promotes visceral fat accumulation, and is associated with a 20% higher CVD mortality risk per daily serving (AHA 2018 advisory).

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High sugar content promotes systemic inflammation via elevated CRP and inflammatory cytokines; phosphoric acid may reduce calcium absorption and worsen RA-related osteoporosis risk
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid regular Coca-Cola; its high sugar load increases inflammatory markers and phosphoric acid content may compound bone mineral loss, particularly in those on corticosteroid therapy.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Coca-Cola is a globally recognised carbonated sugar-sweetened soft drink (SSB) produced by The Coca-Cola Company, originally formulated in the United States in 1886. It is made from carbonated water, sucrose or high-fructose corn syrup, caramel colour (Class IV), phosphoric acid, natural flavours, and caffeine. The beverage is clear amber-brown in colour, intensely sweet with a characteristic cola flavour, and delivers a sharp carbonation sensation. It contains no protein, no fat, and no dietary fibre; its caloric content (42 kcal/100 g) derives entirely from dissolved sugars (9.94 g/100 g). A standard 330 ml can delivers approximately 34 g of free sugar, exceeding the WHO daily free-sugar recommendation of 25 g for adults in a single serving. Coca-Cola has a glycemic index of 63 (medium) and a glycemic load of approximately 22.5 per 330 ml can, making it a significant postprandial glucose challenge. It is widely consumed in Singapore across all demographic groups and is available in regular, Zero Sugar, and Light variants. Clinically, regular Coca-Cola is classified as an ultra-processed sugar-sweetened beverage and is contraindicated or strongly discouraged for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, and rheumatoid arthritis. Its sodium content is low (3 mg/100 g), but the high free-sugar load independently drives blood pressure elevation, insulin resistance, and systemic inflammation. Coca-Cola Zero Sugar is the recommended alternative for those who wish to retain the flavour profile without the glycaemic burden.
common_user_queries: How much sugar is in a can of Coke?
Can I drink Coke if I have high blood pressure?
What is the glycemic index of Coca-Cola?
How many calories in a 330ml can of Coca-Cola?
What is the difference between Coke and Coke Zero?
Can I drink Coke if I have heart disease?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database with confidence 0.95 in the input record; cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central NDB No. 14400 (carbonated cola beverage with caffeine), which reports consistent values. GI sourced from University of Sydney GI Database with a direct match for Coca-Cola regular.
notes: protein_g is confirmed zero by formulation — Coca-Cola contains no protein-bearing ingredients. fat_g is confirmed zero by formulation — Coca-Cola contains no fat-bearing ingredients; saturated, monounsaturated, polyunsaturated, and trans fat are all 0.0 by product formulation, confirmed per HPB and USDA FoodData Central. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero — no fibre-containing ingredients are present. cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero by formulation. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed values for these micronutrients and moisture are not individually reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for Coca-Cola; USDA FoodData Central NDB 14400 reports phosphorus at approximately 11 mg/100 g but this field is retained as -1 pending direct HPB confirmation. Cultural metadata in the input record incorrectly assigned primary_cuisine as 'Chinese' and ethnic_tags as ['Chinese', 'Japanese']; Coca-Cola is a global American brand with no specific Chinese or Japanese cultural origin — corrected to 'Global / International' with region_of_origin 'United States'. Input portions array was empty; serving size set to 330 ml standard can (346 g) based on standard Singapore retail unit. Glycemic load per serving calculated as GI (63) × carbohydrate per serving (35.6 g) / 100 = 22.5.
